---
title: "poc"
author: "smd"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---




```{r}
setwd("~/GitHub/csv_scd2")
library(tidyverse)
library(janitor)
library(logger)
library(digest)


# dummy data for testing
extract1 <- read_csv("test1.csv", col_types = cols(.default = "c")) %>% 
  clean_names()

extract2 <- read_csv("test2.csv", col_types = cols(.default = "c")) %>% 
  clean_names()

```
need to develop a hashing function

table: 
input: key cols
 
```{r}

key_value_hash <- function(df_in, key_cols) {
  all_cols <- df_in %>%  colnames()
  value_cols <- setdiff(all_cols, key_cols)
  
  if (length(all_cols) == length(value_cols)) {
    log_fatal("The key columns do not exist in the table!")
  } else {
    df_out <- df_in %>% 
      unite("key_combined", all_of(key_cols), sep = "|", remove = FALSE) %>% 
      mutate(key_hash = sapply(key_combined, digest, algo = "sha256")) %>% 
      unite("value_combined", all_of(value_cols), sep = "|", remove = FALSE) %>%
      mutate(value_hash = sapply(value_combined, digest, algo = "sha256")) %>% 
      select(-c(key_combined, value_combined))
  
    return(df_out)
  }  
  
}


```

DO WE NEED TO MAKE SURE THAT BOTH S1 AND S2 HAVE THE SAME CORE COLUMNS BEFORE PROCEEDING????

```{r}
ky_cols <- c("name")

S1 <- key_value_hash(extract1, ky_cols) %>% 
  select(key_hash, value_hash) %>% 
  mutate(effective_date = ymd("2020/01/01")) %>% 
  mutate(expiry_date = ymd("9999/12/31"))

S2 <- key_value_hash(extract2, ky_cols) %>% 
  select(key_hash, value_hash) %>% 
  mutate(load_date = ymd("2020/12/31"))

```

```{r}
new_kv_pairs <- anti_join(S2, S1, by = join_by(key_hash, value_hash)) 
```

NEED TO DETECT WHEN KEYS ARE DUPLICATED AND ERROR OUT WHEN THIS HAPPENS!!!!


```{r}
x <- left_join(S1, new_kv_pairs, by = join_by(key_hash))

capped_records <- x %>% 
  mutate(expiry_date = if_else(is.na(load_date), expiry_date, load_date - days())) %>% 
  select(-load_date)
```


```{r}
new_records <- new_kv_pairs %>% 
  rename(effective_date = load_date) %>% 
  mutate(expiry_date = ymd("9999/12/31"))

```


```{r}
y <- bind_rows(capped_records, new_records)
```
